tuv.seg: Buoyant hydrous mantle plume from the mantle transition zone
Panache mantellique hydraté flottant en provenance de la zone de transition du manteau

tu.prop: Buoyant hydrous mantle plume from the mantle transition zone
Scientific Reports volume 9, Article number: 6549 (2019) Cite this article

tuv.seg: Takeshi Kuritani, Qun-Ke Xia, Jun-Ichi Kimura, Jia Liu, Kenji Shimizu, Takayuki Ushikubo, Dapeng Zhao, Mitsuhiro Nakagawa & Shumpei Yoshimura
Takeshi Kuritani, Qun-Ke Xia, Jun-Ichi Kimura, Jia Liu, Kenji Shimizu, Takayuki Ushikubo, Dapeng Zhao, Mitsuhiro Nakagawa et Shumpei Yoshimura

tu.prop: Takeshi Kuritani, Qun-Ke Xia, Jun-Ichi Kimura, Jia Liu, Kenji Shimizu, Takayuki Ushikubo, Dapeng Zhao, Mitsuhiro Nakagawa & Shumpei Yoshimura

Magmatism at some intraplate volcanoes and large igneous provinces (LIPs) in continental areas may originate from hydrous mantle upwelling (i.e. a plume) from the mantle transition zone (MTZ) at 410–660 km depths in the Earth’s deep interior.

tuv.seg: Scientific Reports volume 9, Article number: 6549 (2019) Cite this article
Scientific Reports volume 9, Numéro d'article : 6549 (2019) Citer cet article

tu.prop: Scientific Reports volume 9, Article number: 6549 (2019) Cite this article
However, the ultimate origin of the magmatism, i.e. why mantle plumes could have been generated at the MTZ, remains unclear.

tuv.seg: Magmatism at some intraplate volcanoes and large igneous provinces (LIPs) in continental areas may originate from hydrous mantle upwelling (i.e. a plume) from the mantle transition zone (MTZ) at 410–660 km depths in the Earth’s deep interior.

Le magmatisme de certains volcans intra-plaques et de grandes provinces ignées (GPI) dans les zones continentales peut provenir de la remontée du manteau hydraté (c'est-à-dire un panache) de la zone de transition du manteau (ZTM) à 410-660 km de profondeur dans la Terre profonde.

tu.prop: Magmatism at some intraplate volcanoes and large igneous provinces (LIPs) in continental areas may originate from hydrous mantle upwelling (i.e. a plume) from the mantle transition zone (MTZ) at 410–660 km depths in the Earth’s deep interior.
Here, we study the buoyancy of a plume by investigating basalts from the Changbaishan volcano, beneath which a mantle plume from the hydrous MTZ is observed via seismology.

tuv.seg: However, the ultimate origin of the magmatism, i.e. why mantle plumes could have been generated at the MTZ, remains unclear.
Cependant, l'origine ultime du magmatisme, c'est-à-dire, ce qui explique pourquoi des panaches mantelliques auraient pu être générés à la ZTM, reste floue.

tu.prop: However, the ultimate origin of the magmatism, i.e. why mantle plumes could have been generated at the MTZ, remains unclear.

Based on carefully determined water contents of the basalts, the potential temperature of the source mantle is estimated to be 1310–1400 °C, which is within the range of the normal upper mantle temperature.

tuv.seg: Here, we study the buoyancy of a plume by investigating basalts from the Changbaishan volcano, beneath which a mantle plume from the hydrous MTZ is observed via seismology.
Dans cet article, nous étudions la flottabilité d'un panache en explorant les basaltes du volcan Changbaishan, sous lequel un panache mantellique de la ZTM hydratée est observé par la sismologie.

tu.prop: Here, we study the buoyancy of a plume by investigating basalts from the Changbaishan volcano, beneath which a mantle plume from the hydrous MTZ is observed via seismology.
This observation suggests that the mantle plume did not have a significant excess heat, and that the plume upwelled because of buoyancy resulting from water supplied from the Pacific slab in the MTZ.

tuv.seg: Based on carefully determined water contents of the basalts, the potential temperature of the source mantle is estimated to be 1310–1400 °C, which is within the range of the normal upper mantle temperature.

Sur la base des teneurs en eau soigneusement déterminées des basaltes, la température potentielle du manteau source est estimée entre 1310 et 1400 °C, ce qui se situe dans la plage de la température normale du manteau supérieur.

tu.prop: Based on carefully determined water contents of the basalts, the potential temperature of the source mantle is estimated to be 1310–1400 °C, which is within the range of the normal upper mantle temperature.
Such a hydrous mantle plume can account for the formation of extremely hydrous LIP magmatism.

tuv.seg: This observation suggests that the mantle plume did not have a significant excess heat, and that the plume upwelled because of buoyancy resulting from water supplied from the Pacific slab in the MTZ.

Cette observation suggère que le panache mantellique n'avait pas une chaleur excessive significative, et que le panache remontait en raison de la flottabilité résultant de l'eau fournie à partir de la plaque du Pacifique dans la ZTM.

tu.prop: This observation suggests that the mantle plume did not have a significant excess heat, and that the plume upwelled because of buoyancy resulting from water supplied from the Pacific slab in the MTZ.
The water was originally sourced from a stagnant slab and stored in the MTZ, and then upwelled irrespective of the presence or absence of a deep thermal plume.

tuv.seg: Such a hydrous mantle plume can account for the formation of extremely hydrous LIP magmatism.
Ce panache mantellique hydraté peut expliquer la formation d'un magmatisme extrêmement hydraté des GPI.

tu.prop: Such a hydrous mantle plume can account for the formation of extremely hydrous LIP magmatism.

tuv.seg: The water was originally sourced from a stagnant slab and stored in the MTZ, and then upwelled irrespective of the presence or absence of a deep thermal plume.
L'eau provenait à l'origine d'une plaque stagnante et stockée dans la ZTM, puis remontée indépendamment de la présence ou de l'absence d'un panache thermique profond.

tu.prop: The water was originally sourced from a stagnant slab and stored in the MTZ, and then upwelled irrespective of the presence or absence of a deep thermal plume.

Mantle plumes associated with magmatism at hotspots and large igneous provinces (LIPs) are commonly thought to be driven by thermal buoyancy originating from the steep thermal gradient at the core–mantle boundary1,2, as evidenced from their high mantle potential temperature3,4,5,6.

tuv.seg: Introduction
Introduction

tu.prop: In addition to this deeply rooted thermal plume model, there is growing evidence that some mantle plumes are generated from the hydrous mantle transition zone (MTZ) at 410–660 km depth that borders the upper and lower mantles.

tuv.seg: Mantle plumes associated with magmatism at hotspots and large igneous provinces (LIPs) are commonly thought to be driven by thermal buoyancy originating from the steep thermal gradient at the core–mantle boundary1,2, as evidenced from their high mantle potential temperature3,4,5,6.

On considère souvent que les panaches mantelliques associés au magmatisme dans les points chauds et les grandes provinces ignées (GPI) sont entraînés par la flottabilité thermique provenant du gradient thermique abrupt à la limite noyau-manteau, comme en témoigne la température potentielle élevée de leur manteau.

tu.prop: Mantle plumes associated with magmatism at hotspots and large igneous provinces (LIPs) are commonly thought to be driven by thermal buoyancy originating from the steep thermal gradient at the core–mantle boundary1,2, as evidenced from their high mantle potential temperature3,4,5,6.
These examples are always large intraplate volcanoes7, flood basalts8, and LIPs9 in continental areas.

tuv.seg: In addition to this deeply rooted thermal plume model, there is growing evidence that some mantle plumes are generated from the hydrous mantle transition zone (MTZ) at 410–660 km depth that borders the upper and lower mantles.

En plus de ce modèle de panache thermique profondément enraciné, il y a de plus en plus de preuves que certains panaches mantelliques sont générés à partir de la zone de transition du manteau (ZTM) hydraté à 410–660 km de profondeur qui borde les manteaux supérieur et inférieur.

tu.prop: In addition to this deeply rooted thermal plume model, there is growing evidence that some mantle plumes are generated from the hydrous mantle transition zone (MTZ) at 410–660 km depth that borders the upper and lower mantles.
Effusions of large amounts of volatile-rich magmas on-land must have caused prominent gas emission and severely impacted the Earth’s surface, which led to mass extinction in some cases10.

tuv.seg: These examples are always large intraplate volcanoes7, flood basalts8, and LIPs9 in continental areas.
Ces exemples sont toujours des volcans intra-plaques volumineux, des basaltes de plateau et de GPI dans les zones continentales.

tu.prop: These examples are always large intraplate volcanoes7, flood basalts8, and LIPs9 in continental areas.
Despite their significance in the Earth sciences, it remains unclear why such mantle plumes developed at the MTZ beneath continents.

tuv.seg: Effusions of large amounts of volatile-rich magmas on-land must have caused prominent gas emission and severely impacted the Earth’s surface, which led to mass extinction in some cases10.

Les épanchements de grandes quantités de magmas riches en substances volatiles sur la Terre ont dû provoquer d'importantes émissions de gaz et avoir de graves répercussions sur la surface de la Terre, entraînant dans certains cas des extinctions massives.

tu.prop: Effusions of large amounts of volatile-rich magmas on-land must have caused prominent gas emission and severely impacted the Earth’s surface, which led to mass extinction in some cases10.
Water is clearly a plausible source for the buoyancy of a hydrous mantle plume9,11,12.

tuv.seg: Despite their significance in the Earth sciences, it remains unclear why such mantle plumes developed at the MTZ beneath continents.
Malgré leur importance dans les sciences de la Terre, on ne sait toujours pas pourquoi ces panaches mantelliques se sont développés dans la ZTM sous les continents.

tu.prop: Despite their significance in the Earth sciences, it remains unclear why such mantle plumes developed at the MTZ beneath continents.

However, whether the plume generation resulted solely from compositional buoyancy, or additional thermal buoyancy was required, has not been critically evaluated for any mantle plumes that evidently derived from the MTZ.

tuv.seg: Water is clearly a plausible source for the buoyancy of a hydrous mantle plume9,11,12.
L'eau est manifestement une source plausible de flottabilité d'un panache mantellique hydraté.

tu.prop: Water is clearly a plausible source for the buoyancy of a hydrous mantle plume9,11,12.
Ancient magmatism is a less suitable target to evaluate buoyancy as we lack seismic data regarding mantle velocities.

tuv.seg: However, whether the plume generation resulted solely from compositional buoyancy, or additional thermal buoyancy was required, has not been critically evaluated for any mantle plumes that evidently derived from the MTZ.

Toutefois, il n'a pas été possible d'évaluer de manière critique si la formation du panache résultait uniquement de la flottabilité de la composition ou si une flottabilité thermique supplémentaire était nécessaire pour les panaches mantelliques qui ont manifestement dérivé de la ZTM.

tu.prop: However, whether the plume generation resulted solely from compositional buoyancy, or additional thermal buoyancy was required, has not been critically evaluated for any mantle plumes that evidently derived from the MTZ.
Changbaishan is among the best suited volcanoes for the study of a possible mantle plume from the hydrous MTZ.

tuv.seg: Ancient magmatism is a less suitable target to evaluate buoyancy as we lack seismic data regarding mantle velocities.
Le magmatisme ancien est une cible moins appropriée pour évaluer la flottabilité, car nous manquons de données sismiques concernant les vitesses du manteau.

tu.prop: Ancient magmatism is a less suitable target to evaluate buoyancy as we lack seismic data regarding mantle velocities.
This volcano, located on the border between China and North Korea, is a continental intraplate volcano forming one of the largest active volcanic fields in Northeast Asia (Fig. 1).

tuv.seg: Changbaishan is among the best suited volcanoes for the study of a possible mantle plume from the hydrous MTZ.
Le Changbaishan est l'un des volcans les mieux adaptés à l'étude d'un éventuel panache mantellique de la ZTM hydratée.

tu.prop: Changbaishan is among the best suited volcanoes for the study of a possible mantle plume from the hydrous MTZ.
The volcanism has been sustained for >20 Myrs13 and has produced a basaltic lava plateau covering ~12,000 km2 14.

tuv.seg: This volcano, located on the border between China and North Korea, is a continental intraplate volcano forming one of the largest active volcanic fields in Northeast Asia (Fig. 1).
Ce volcan, situé à la frontière entre la Chine et la Corée du Nord, est un volcan intraplaque continental formant l'un des plus grands champs volcaniques actifs en Asie du Nord-Est (Fig. 1).

tu.prop: This volcano, located on the border between China and North Korea, is a continental intraplate volcano forming one of the largest active volcanic fields in Northeast Asia (Fig. 1).
A caldera-forming eruption that occurred during the 10th century was among the largest eruptions on Earth during the past 2,000 years13.

tuv.seg: The volcanism has been sustained for >20 Myrs13 and has produced a basaltic lava plateau covering ~12,000 km2 14.
Le volcanisme s'est maintenu pendant plus de 20 Ma et a produit un plateau de laves basaltiques couvrant ~12 000 km2.

tu.prop: The volcanism has been sustained for >20 Myrs13 and has produced a basaltic lava plateau covering ~12,000 km2 14.
Seismological studies have shown that the subducting Pacific slab is stagnant in the MTZ beneath the entire area of Eastern China7,15 (Fig. 1).

tuv.seg: A caldera-forming eruption that occurred during the 10th century was among the largest eruptions on Earth during the past 2,000 years13.
Une éruption formant des caldeiras qui s'est produite au Xe siècle a été l'une des plus grandes éruptions sur Terre au cours des 2 000 dernières années.

tu.prop: A caldera-forming eruption that occurred during the 10th century was among the largest eruptions on Earth during the past 2,000 years13.
The MTZ here is considerably hydrous in comparison to the globally dry MTZ as shown by electrical conductivity observations16,17.

tuv.seg: Seismological studies have shown that the subducting Pacific slab is stagnant in the MTZ beneath the entire area of Eastern China7,15 (Fig. 1).
Des données sismologiques ont révélé que la plaque Pacifique plongeante stagne dans la ZTM sous toute la surface de l’Est de la Chine (Figure 1).

tu.prop: Seismological studies have shown that the subducting Pacific slab is stagnant in the MTZ beneath the entire area of Eastern China7,15 (Fig. 1).

Beneath Changbaishan, a prominent low-velocity anomaly with a plume-like shape is observed across the upper mantle using P-wave seismic tomography7 (Fig. 1), which is among the most clearly imaged examples of a mantle plume originating from the MTZ.

tuv.seg: The MTZ here is considerably hydrous in comparison to the globally dry MTZ as shown by electrical conductivity observations16,17.
Comme le montrent les mesures de conductivité électrique, la ZTM est nettement plus hydratée ici que la ZTM sèche à l’échelle de la planète.

tu.prop: The MTZ here is considerably hydrous in comparison to the globally dry MTZ as shown by electrical conductivity observations16,17.

Geochemical studies of the basalts also show involvement of the stagnant slab and metasomatised peridotite materials from the MTZ in their mantle source18,19, yet water content in the mantle source has not been accurately determined.

tuv.seg: Beneath Changbaishan, a prominent low-velocity anomaly with a plume-like shape is observed across the upper mantle using P-wave seismic tomography7 (Fig. 1), which is among the most clearly imaged examples of a mantle plume originating from the MTZ.

Sous le Changbaishan, une importante anomalie à faible vitesse avec une forme de panache est observée à travers le manteau supérieur à l'aide de la tomographie sismique à ondes P (figure 1), qui compte parmi les exemples les plus clairement imagés d'un panache mantellique provenant de la ZTM.

tu.prop: Beneath Changbaishan, a prominent low-velocity anomaly with a plume-like shape is observed across the upper mantle using P-wave seismic tomography7 (Fig. 1), which is among the most clearly imaged examples of a mantle plume originating from the MTZ.
In this study, the water content of the basalt was determined using thermodynamic analyses and mineral melt inclusions for selected young basaltic samples.

tuv.seg: Geochemical studies of the basalts also show involvement of the stagnant slab and metasomatised peridotite materials from the MTZ in their mantle source18,19, yet water content in the mantle source has not been accurately determined.

Plusieurs études géochimiques des basaltes montrent également l'implication de la plaque stagnante et des matériaux de péridotites métasomatisés de la ZTM dans leur source mantellique. Toutefois, la teneur en eau dans la source mantellique n'a pas été déterminée avec précision.

tu.prop: Geochemical studies of the basalts also show involvement of the stagnant slab and metasomatised peridotite materials from the MTZ in their mantle source18,19, yet water content in the mantle source has not been accurately determined.

We use such measured water contents to estimate mantle potential temperatures using two independent models20,21, and we finally examine the source of the buoyancy of the mantle plume beneath the Changbaishan volcano.

tuv.seg: In this study, the water content of the basalt was determined using thermodynamic analyses and mineral melt inclusions for selected young basaltic samples.

Dans cette étude, la teneur en eau des basaltes a pu être déterminée grâce aux analyses thermodynamiques et aux inclusions vitreuses minérales pour un certain nombre d’échantillons de basaltes récents.

tu.prop: In this study, the water content of the basalt was determined using thermodynamic analyses and mineral melt inclusions for selected young basaltic samples.

tuv.seg: We use such measured water contents to estimate mantle potential temperatures using two independent models20,21, and we finally examine the source of the buoyancy of the mantle plume beneath the Changbaishan volcano.

Nous utilisons cette teneur en eau mesurée afin d’évaluer les températures potentielles du manteau en utilisant deux modèles indépendants, avant d'étudier l’origine de la flottabilité du panache mantellique sous le volcan du Changbaishan.

tu.prop: We use such measured water contents to estimate mantle potential temperatures using two independent models20,21, and we finally examine the source of the buoyancy of the mantle plume beneath the Changbaishan volcano.

Les tâches grises désignent les champs volcaniques représentatifs du Cénozoïque en Chine.

tuv.seg: The solid and dashed contour lines denote depths to the upper boundaries of the subducting Pacific and Philippine Sea slabs, respectively51.

Les lignes de contour continues et discontinues représentent respectivement les profondeurs à des limites supérieures de la plaque plongeante Pacifique et de la plaque plongeante de la mer des Philippines.

Les lignes rouges représentent les géométries des discontinuités à 410 et 660 km, estimées à l’aide d’une analyse par fonction récepteur télésismique.

tu.prop: % SiO2), with scarce andesitic lavas (Supplementary Fig. S1).

tuv.seg: The products of the Changbaishan volcano exhibit a wide compositional range from basalt to rhyolite (46–75 wt.
Les produits du volcan de Changbaishan présentent une grande variété de composition, allant des basaltes aux rhyolites (46-75 % en poids

tu.prop: The products of the Changbaishan volcano exhibit a wide compositional range from basalt to rhyolite (46–75 wt.
During the activity of the volcano, basaltic magmas have been most common, although felsic magmas have dominated since ~3 Ma13.

tuv.seg: % SiO2), with scarce andesitic lavas (Supplementary Fig. S1).
de SiO2) et de rares laves andésitiques (voir figure supplémentaire S1).

tu.prop: % SiO2), with scarce andesitic lavas (Supplementary Fig. S1).
The basaltic magmas are primarily alkaline, but some tholeiitic (subalkaline) basalts and basaltic andesites also occur (Supplementary Fig. S1).

tuv.seg: During the activity of the volcano, basaltic magmas have been most common, although felsic magmas have dominated since ~3 Ma13.
Pendant l’activité volcanique, les magmas basaltiques sont les plus communs, bien que les magmas felsiques aient dominé depuis 3 Ma.

tu.prop: During the activity of the volcano, basaltic magmas have been most common, although felsic magmas have dominated since ~3 Ma13.
The alkalic and tholeiitic basalts have similar Fe2O3* contents at a given MgO content (Supplementary Fig. S2).

tuv.seg: The basaltic magmas are primarily alkaline, but some tholeiitic (subalkaline) basalts and basaltic andesites also occur (Supplementary Fig. S1).
Les magmas basaltiques sont essentiellement alcalins, mais on trouve également certains basaltes tholéiitiques (subalcalins), ainsi que des andésites basaltiques (voir figure supplémentaire S1).

tu.prop: The basaltic magmas are primarily alkaline, but some tholeiitic (subalkaline) basalts and basaltic andesites also occur (Supplementary Fig. S1).
The chemistry of incompatible trace elements has an affinity to intra-plate basalt but with significant elevations in Ba, Pb, and Sr concentrations (Supplementary Fig. S3a), which are fluid mobile.

tuv.seg: The alkalic and tholeiitic basalts have similar Fe2O3* contents at a given MgO content (Supplementary Fig. S2).
Les basaltes alcalins et tholéiitiques ont des teneurs en Fe2O3* similaires pour une teneur en MgO donnée (voir figure supplémentaire S2).

tu.prop: The alkalic and tholeiitic basalts have similar Fe2O3* contents at a given MgO content (Supplementary Fig. S2).
The alkaline basalts have relatively high concentrations of incompatible elements compared to the tholeiitic basalts.

tuv.seg: The chemistry of incompatible trace elements has an affinity to intra-plate basalt but with significant elevations in Ba, Pb, and Sr concentrations (Supplementary Fig. S3a), which are fluid mobile.

La chimie des éléments en traces incompatibles présente une affinité pour les basaltes intra-plaques mais accompagnés d’élévations importantes en concentrations de Ba, Pb et Sr (voir figure S3a), qui sont des fluides mobiles.

tu.prop: The chemistry of incompatible trace elements has an affinity to intra-plate basalt but with significant elevations in Ba, Pb, and Sr concentrations (Supplementary Fig. S3a), which are fluid mobile.
Water content of the Changbaishan basalts have yet to be successfully measured, because the basalts have been subaerially emplaced and so they are commonly degassed.

tuv.seg: The alkaline basalts have relatively high concentrations of incompatible elements compared to the tholeiitic basalts.
Les basaltes alcalins ont des concentrations relativement élevées en éléments incompatibles en comparaison avec les basaltes tholéiitiques.

tu.prop: The alkaline basalts have relatively high concentrations of incompatible elements compared to the tholeiitic basalts.

Our preliminary analyses showed that pyroxene and plagioclase phenocrysts and melt inclusions in minerals underwent a significant loss of water, even in the quenched scoria samples, probably due to slow ascent of the magmas through thick continental lithosphere (~100 km thick22).

tuv.seg: Water content of the Changbaishan basalts have yet to be successfully measured, because the basalts have been subaerially emplaced and so they are commonly degassed.
La teneur en eau des basaltes de Changbaishan n’a pas encore été mesurée correctement, car ils ont été mis en place de manière subaérienne et sont donc souvent dégazés.

tu.prop: Water content of the Changbaishan basalts have yet to be successfully measured, because the basalts have been subaerially emplaced and so they are commonly degassed.
In this study, water content was estimated by applying plagioclase-melt hygrometry on a young (<1 Ma) trachy-basalt scoria sample, H2505 (Supplementary Fig. S4; Supplementary Table S1).

tuv.seg: Our preliminary analyses showed that pyroxene and plagioclase phenocrysts and melt inclusions in minerals underwent a significant loss of water, even in the quenched scoria samples, probably due to slow ascent of the magmas through thick continental lithosphere (~100 km thick22).

Nos analyses préliminaires ont montré que les phénocristaux de pyroxène et de plagioclase et les inclusions magmatiques minérales ont subi une perte en eau significative, et ce, même dans les échantillons de scories trempés, ce qui est probablement dû à une remontée lente des magmas à travers l’épaisse lithosphère continentale (~100 km d'épaisseur).

tu.prop: Our preliminary analyses showed that pyroxene and plagioclase phenocrysts and melt inclusions in minerals underwent a significant loss of water, even in the quenched scoria samples, probably due to slow ascent of the magmas through thick continental lithosphere (~100 km thick22).

Similarly, the minimum water content of basaltic melt was also measured by analyses of melt inclusions contained in olivine phenocrysts in a basaltic trachy-andesite sample, H90713 (Supplementary Fig. S5; Supplementary Table S1).

tuv.seg: In this study, water content was estimated by applying plagioclase-melt hygrometry on a young (<1 Ma) trachy-basalt scoria sample, H2505 (Supplementary Fig. S4; Supplementary Table S1).

Dans cette étude, la teneur en eau a été estimée en appliquant l'hygrométrie du magma de plagioclase sur un échantillon de scorie trachy-basaltique jeune (<1 Ma), H2505 (cf. figure supplémentaire S4 ; tableau supplémentaire S1).

tu.prop: In this study, water content was estimated by applying plagioclase-melt hygrometry on a young (<1 Ma) trachy-basalt scoria sample, H2505 (Supplementary Fig. S4; Supplementary Table S1).
This sample was collected from the Plinian fall deposits (Unit B of Phase 2) of the 10th century caldera-forming eruption23.

Based on the constraint of 1.4–2.2 wt.% obtained by the plagioclase–melt hygrometry and that of > ~1.5 wt.% by the SIMS analysis, we deem that the water content of the H2505 magma was 1.5–2.2 wt.

tuv.seg: Therefore, the H2O content of ~1.8 ± 0.4 wt.% estimated using plagioclase–melt hygrometry for H2505 is consistent with > ~1.5 wt.% from the melt inclusion in H90173.

Par conséquent, la teneur en H2O de ~ 1,8 ± 0,4 % en poids estimée à l'aide de l'hygrométrie du magma de plagioclase pour l'échantillon H2505 est cohérente avec une teneur > ~ 1,5 % en poids provenant de l'inclusion magmatique dans l'échantillon H90173.

tu.prop: Therefore, the H2O content of ~1.8 ± 0.4 wt.% estimated using plagioclase–melt hygrometry for H2505 is consistent with > ~1.5 wt.% from the melt inclusion in H90173.
The phenocryst assemblage of H2505 is olivine and plagioclase, and no significant Eu anomaly is observed in the rare-earth-element pattern of the whole-rock sample (Supplementary Fig. S3b).

tuv.seg: Based on the constraint of 1.4–2.2 wt.% obtained by the plagioclase–melt hygrometry and that of > ~1.5 wt.% by the SIMS analysis, we deem that the water content of the H2505 magma was 1.5–2.2 wt.

Sur la base de la contrainte de 1,4 à 2,2 % en poids obtenue par l'hygrométrie du magma de plagioclase et celle de > ~ 1,5 % en poids par l'analyse SIMS, nous estimons que la teneur en eau du magma H2505 était de 1,5 à 2,2 % en poids.

Therefore, it can be considered that the H2505 magma derived from the primary magma solely by olivine fractionation.

tuv.seg: The phenocryst assemblage of H2505 is olivine and plagioclase, and no significant Eu anomaly is observed in the rare-earth-element pattern of the whole-rock sample (Supplementary Fig. S3b).

L'assemblage de phénocristaux de H2505 est composée d'olivine et de plagioclase, et aucune anomalie significative de Eu n'est observée dans le schéma des terres rares de l'échantillon de roche totale (cf. figure supplémentaire S3b).

tu.prop: The phenocryst assemblage of H2505 is olivine and plagioclase, and no significant Eu anomaly is observed in the rare-earth-element pattern of the whole-rock sample (Supplementary Fig. S3b).
The majority of Fo content [Mg/(Mg + Fe) × 100] of olivine crystals in peridotite xenoliths from Changbaishan, which may represent newly accreted lithospheric mantle underneath the craton, is ~9026.

tuv.seg: Therefore, it can be considered that the H2505 magma derived from the primary magma solely by olivine fractionation.
Par conséquent, on peut considérer que le magma H2505 dérive du magma primaire uniquement par fractionnement de l'olivine.

tu.prop: Therefore, it can be considered that the H2505 magma derived from the primary magma solely by olivine fractionation.

Therefore, given that the primary magma was in equilibrium with the mantle olivine with Fo content of 90, the primary magmatic composition was calculated using the olivine maximum fractionation model27 (Supplementary Table S1; Supplementary Fig. S8).

tuv.seg: The majority of Fo content [Mg/(Mg + Fe) × 100] of olivine crystals in peridotite xenoliths from Changbaishan, which may represent newly accreted lithospheric mantle underneath the craton, is ~9026.

La majorité de la teneur en Fo [Mg/(Mg + Fe) × 100] des cristaux d'olivine dans les xénolites de péridotite de Changbaishan, qui pourraient représenter un manteau lithosphérique récemment accrété sous le craton, est d'environ 90 26.

tu.prop: The majority of Fo content [Mg/(Mg + Fe) × 100] of olivine crystals in peridotite xenoliths from Changbaishan, which may represent newly accreted lithospheric mantle underneath the craton, is ~9026.
The trace element and water content of the primary magma were calculated assuming that the concentrations of these elements were negligible in the fractionated olivine.

tuv.seg: Therefore, given that the primary magma was in equilibrium with the mantle olivine with Fo content of 90, the primary magmatic composition was calculated using the olivine maximum fractionation model27 (Supplementary Table S1; Supplementary Fig. S8).

Par conséquent, étant donné que le magma primaire était en équilibre avec l'olivine mantellique avec une teneur en Fo de 90, la composition magmatique primaire a été calculée à l'aide du modèle de fractionnement maximal de l'olivine27 (cf. tableau supplémentaire S1 ; figure supplémentaire S8).

tu.prop: Therefore, given that the primary magma was in equilibrium with the mantle olivine with Fo content of 90, the primary magmatic composition was calculated using the olivine maximum fractionation model27 (Supplementary Table S1; Supplementary Fig. S8).
Finally, the water content of the primary alkaline basaltic magma was estimated to be 1.2–1.8 wt.

tuv.seg: The trace element and water content of the primary magma were calculated assuming that the concentrations of these elements were negligible in the fractionated olivine.
La teneur en éléments-traces et en eau du magma primaire a été calculée en supposant que les concentrations de ces éléments étaient négligeables dans l'olivine fractionnée.

tu.prop: The trace element and water content of the primary magma were calculated assuming that the concentrations of these elements were negligible in the fractionated olivine.
This result can be tested further by the water contents of mineral melt inclusions in the felsic products from Changbaishan28.

tuv.seg: Finally, the water content of the primary alkaline basaltic magma was estimated to be 1.2–1.8 wt.
Enfin, la teneur en eau du magma basaltique alcalin primaire a été estimée à 1,2-1,8 % en poids.

tu.prop: Finally, the water content of the primary alkaline basaltic magma was estimated to be 1.2–1.8 wt.
The felsic magmas evolved from a parental alkaline basaltic magma primarily by fractional crystallisation without significant crustal assimilation23.

tuv.seg: This result can be tested further by the water contents of mineral melt inclusions in the felsic products from Changbaishan28.
Ce résultat peut être vérifié davantage par les teneurs en eau des inclusions minérales dans les produits felsiques de Changbaishan28.

tu.prop: This result can be tested further by the water contents of mineral melt inclusions in the felsic products from Changbaishan28.

Given that the water content of the melt inclusions with the highest H2O/Zr ratio represents the least water leakage from the inclusion, the water content in a primary alkaline basaltic magma is estimated to be >1.2 wt.% using the H2O/Zr ratio of 0.00628 and a Zr content of primary basaltic magma of 207 ppm (Supplementary Table S1).

tuv.seg: The felsic magmas evolved from a parental alkaline basaltic magma primarily by fractional crystallisation without significant crustal assimilation23.
Les magmas felsiques ont évolué à partir d'un magma basaltique alcalin parental principalement par cristallisation fractionnée sans assimilation crustale significative23.

tu.prop: The felsic magmas evolved from a parental alkaline basaltic magma primarily by fractional crystallisation without significant crustal assimilation23.
This result is in good agreement with the estimate of our study.

tuv.seg: Given that the water content of the melt inclusions with the highest H2O/Zr ratio represents the least water leakage from the inclusion, the water content in a primary alkaline basaltic magma is estimated to be >1.2 wt.% using the H2O/Zr ratio of 0.00628 and a Zr content of primary basaltic magma of 207 ppm (Supplementary Table S1).

Étant donné que la teneur en eau des inclusions magmatiques avec le rapport H2O/Zr le plus élevé représente la plus petite fuite d'eau de l'inclusion, la teneur en eau dans un magma basaltique alcalin primaire est estimée à >1,2 % en poids en utilisant le rapport H2O/Zr de 0,00628 et une teneur en Zr du magma basaltique primaire de 207 ppm (cf. tableau supplémentaire S1).

tu.prop: Given that the water content of the melt inclusions with the highest H2O/Zr ratio represents the least water leakage from the inclusion, the water content in a primary alkaline basaltic magma is estimated to be >1.2 wt.% using the H2O/Zr ratio of 0.00628 and a Zr content of primary basaltic magma of 207 ppm (Supplementary Table S1).

Melting conditions of the source mantle for the Changbaishan basalts were determined by applying the Ocean Basalt Simulator version 1 model (OBS1)20 using the estimated trace element and water contents of the primary H2505 alkaline basaltic magma (Supplementary Table S1).

tuv.seg: This result is in good agreement with the estimate of our study.
Ce résultat est concluant avec l'estimation de notre étude.

tu.prop: This result is in good agreement with the estimate of our study.

The analytical results show that (1) the fraction of the pyroxenite component in the source mantle was 4–11%; (2) the degree of melting was 3–6%; (3) the water content in the source mantle was 350–550 ppm; (4) the pressure and temperature of the melt segregation were 2.6–2.8 GPa and 1340–1380 °C, respectively; and (5) the mantle potential temperature was 1314–1357 °C (Supplementary Methods).

tuv.seg: Melting conditions of the source mantle for the Changbaishan basalts were determined by applying the Ocean Basalt Simulator version 1 model (OBS1)20 using the estimated trace element and water contents of the primary H2505 alkaline basaltic magma (Supplementary Table S1).

Les conditions de fusion du manteau source pour les basaltes de Changbaishan ont été déterminées en appliquant le modèle Ocean Basalt Simulator version 1 (OBS1)20 en utilisant les estimations des teneurs en éléments-traces et en eau du magma basaltique alcalin primaire H2505 (cf. tableau supplémentaire S1).

tu.prop: Melting conditions of the source mantle for the Changbaishan basalts were determined by applying the Ocean Basalt Simulator version 1 model (OBS1)20 using the estimated trace element and water contents of the primary H2505 alkaline basaltic magma (Supplementary Table S1).
It is noteworthy that the water content of the source mantle of 350–550 ppm is significantly higher than that of a normal mid-ocean ridge basalt (N-MORB) source mantle of 120 ppm29.

tuv.seg: The analytical results show that (1) the fraction of the pyroxenite component in the source mantle was 4–11%; (2) the degree of melting was 3–6%; (3) the water content in the source mantle was 350–550 ppm; (4) the pressure and temperature of the melt segregation were 2.6–2.8 GPa and 1340–1380 °C, respectively; and (5) the mantle potential temperature was 1314–1357 °C (Supplementary Methods).

Les résultats analytiques montrent que (1) la fraction du composant pyroxénite dans le manteau source était de 4 à 11 % ; (2) le degré de fusion était de 3 à 6 % ; (3) la teneur en eau dans le manteau source était de 350 à 550 ppm ; (4) la pression et la température de la ségrégation de la fonte étaient, respectivement, de 2,6 à 2,8 GPa et de 1340 à 1380 °C ; et (5) la température potentielle du manteau était de 1314 à 1357 °C (cf. méthodes supplémentaires).

tu.prop: The analytical results show that (1) the fraction of the pyroxenite component in the source mantle was 4–11%; (2) the degree of melting was 3–6%; (3) the water content in the source mantle was 350–550 ppm; (4) the pressure and temperature of the melt segregation were 2.6–2.8 GPa and 1340–1380 °C, respectively; and (5) the mantle potential temperature was 1314–1357 °C (Supplementary Methods).

To check the aforementioned results obtained by OBS1, the melting temperature of the primary magma was independently estimated using the major element composition and the water content (Supplementary Table S1).

tuv.seg: It is noteworthy that the water content of the source mantle of 350–550 ppm is significantly higher than that of a normal mid-ocean ridge basalt (N-MORB) source mantle of 120 ppm29.

Il faut noter que la teneur en eau du manteau source de 350 à 550 ppm est significativement plus élevée que celle du manteau source basaltique de la dorsale médio-océanique normale (N-MORB) de 120 ppm29.

tu.prop: It is noteworthy that the water content of the source mantle of 350–550 ppm is significantly higher than that of a normal mid-ocean ridge basalt (N-MORB) source mantle of 120 ppm29.
The olivine liquidus temperature for the anhydrous primary magma was calculated using the alphaMELTS model operated in the pMELTS mode30,31.

tuv.seg: To check the aforementioned results obtained by OBS1, the melting temperature of the primary magma was independently estimated using the major element composition and the water content (Supplementary Table S1).

Pour vérifier les résultats susmentionnés obtenus par OBS1, la température de fusion du magma primaire a été estimée indépendamment en utilisant la composition des éléments majeurs et la teneur en eau (cf. tableau supplémentaire S1).

tu.prop: To check the aforementioned results obtained by OBS1, the melting temperature of the primary magma was independently estimated using the major element composition and the water content (Supplementary Table S1).
The calculated temperature was then corrected to obtain the liquidus temperature for the hydrous primary magma containing 1.2–1.8 wt.

tu.prop: The olivine liquidus temperature for the anhydrous primary magma was calculated using the alphaMELTS model operated in the pMELTS mode30,31.

La température estimée pour le magma primaire était de 1357 à 1387 °C à 2,6-2,8 GPa (la pression de l'OBS1).

The mantle potential temperature was also estimated using the model of ref.21.

tuv.seg: This is in good agreement with the estimated melting temperatures of 1340–1380 °C using OBS1.
Cela concorde avec les températures de fusion estimées de 1340 à 1380 °C à l'aide d'OBS1.

tu.prop: This is in good agreement with the estimated melting temperatures of 1340–1380 °C using OBS1.
The model results showed that, for water contents of the primary magma of 1.2 wt.% and 1.8 wt.%, the mantle potential temperatures were 1372 ± 32 °C and 1356 ± 25 °C, respectively.

tuv.seg: The mantle potential temperature was also estimated using the model of ref.21.
La température potentielle du manteau a également été estimée en utilisant le modèle fourni en réf.21.

tu.prop: The mantle potential temperature was also estimated using the model of ref.21.

tuv.seg: The model results showed that, for water contents of the primary magma of 1.2 wt.% and 1.8 wt.%, the mantle potential temperatures were 1372 ± 32 °C and 1356 ± 25 °C, respectively.

Les résultats du modèle ont montré que, pour des teneurs en eau du magma primaire de 1,2 % en poids et 1,8 % en poids, les températures potentielles du manteau étaient respectivement de 1372 ± 32 °C et 1356 ± 25 °C.

tu.prop: The model results showed that, for water contents of the primary magma of 1.2 wt.% and 1.8 wt.%, the mantle potential temperatures were 1372 ± 32 °C and 1356 ± 25 °C, respectively.
Geophysical studies have suggested that the mantle plume beneath the Changbaishan volcano is derived from the MTZ7.

tuv.seg: Discussion
Discussion

tu.prop: However, the source of the buoyancy of the mantle plume, i.e. thermal33, compositional11,18,34, or both7,35, remains controversial, because the water contents of the source mantle have yet to be reliably estimated.

tuv.seg: Geophysical studies have suggested that the mantle plume beneath the Changbaishan volcano is derived from the MTZ7.
Des études géophysiques ont suggéré que le panache mantellique sous le volcan du Changbaishan est dérivé du MTZ7.

tu.prop: Geophysical studies have suggested that the mantle plume beneath the Changbaishan volcano is derived from the MTZ7.
We estimated the mantle potential temperature for the plume as 1310–1360 °C by OBS1 and 1330–1400 °C by the model of ref.21, considering water.

tuv.seg: However, the source of the buoyancy of the mantle plume, i.e. thermal33, compositional11,18,34, or both7,35, remains controversial, because the water contents of the source mantle have yet to be reliably estimated.

Cependant, la source de la flottabilité du panache mantellique, c'est-à-dire thermique33, compositionnelle11,18,34, ou les deux7,35, reste controversée, car les teneurs en eau du manteau source n'ont pas encore été estimées de manière fiable.

tu.prop: However, the source of the buoyancy of the mantle plume, i.e. thermal33, compositional11,18,34, or both7,35, remains controversial, because the water contents of the source mantle have yet to be reliably estimated.
These temperature ranges are essentially within the range of 1300–1400 °C for MORB4,5,6, which represents the temperatures of the normal ambient upper mantle.

tuv.seg: We estimated the mantle potential temperature for the plume as 1310–1360 °C by OBS1 and 1330–1400 °C by the model of ref.21, considering water.
Nous avons estimé la température potentielle du manteau pour le panache à 1310–1360 °C avec l'OBS1 et à 1330-1400 °C avec le modèle de la réf.21, en tenant compte de l'eau.

tu.prop: We estimated the mantle potential temperature for the plume as 1310–1360 °C by OBS1 and 1330–1400 °C by the model of ref.21, considering water.

This observation suggests that the Changbaishan mantle plume is not significantly excessively hot compared to the temperature of the ambient mantle, and therefore, we conclude that thermal buoyancy is not a primary cause of the mantle plume at least for the youngest activity of Changbaishan which corresponds with the present-day snapshot of the seismologically detected mantle plume.

tuv.seg: These temperature ranges are essentially within the range of 1300–1400 °C for MORB4,5,6, which represents the temperatures of the normal ambient upper mantle.
Ces plages de température se situent essentiellement dans la plage de 1300–1400 °C pour le MORB4,5,6, ce qui représente les températures du manteau supérieur ambiant normal.

tu.prop: These temperature ranges are essentially within the range of 1300–1400 °C for MORB4,5,6, which represents the temperatures of the normal ambient upper mantle.

We note that the magmatism of the alkaline trachy basalt, for which we estimated the water content using SIMS, was active ~1000 years ago, and therefore, the present-day mantle potential temperature is well represented by the estimate of 1310–1400 °C.

tuv.seg: This observation suggests that the Changbaishan mantle plume is not significantly excessively hot compared to the temperature of the ambient mantle, and therefore, we conclude that thermal buoyancy is not a primary cause of the mantle plume at least for the youngest activity of Changbaishan which corresponds with the present-day snapshot of the seismologically detected mantle plume.

Cette observation suggère que le panache mantellique de Changbaishan n'est pas significativement trop chaud par rapport à la température du manteau ambiant, et par conséquent, nous concluons que la flottabilité thermique n'est pas une cause principale du panache mantellique, au moins pour l'activité la plus jeune de Changbaishan qui correspond à l'instantané actuel du panache mantellique détecté sismiquement.

tu.prop: This observation suggests that the Changbaishan mantle plume is not significantly excessively hot compared to the temperature of the ambient mantle, and therefore, we conclude that thermal buoyancy is not a primary cause of the mantle plume at least for the youngest activity of Changbaishan which corresponds with the present-day snapshot of the seismologically detected mantle plume.
The source mantle of the Changbaishan basalts is considered to contain some sediment component from the subducted Pacific slab18.

tuv.seg: We note that the magmatism of the alkaline trachy basalt, for which we estimated the water content using SIMS, was active ~1000 years ago, and therefore, the present-day mantle potential temperature is well represented by the estimate of 1310–1400 °C.

Nous notons que le magmatisme du trachybasalte alcalin, pour lequel nous avons estimé la teneur en eau à l'aide de SIMS, était actif il y a ~1000 ans, et par conséquent, la température potentielle actuelle du manteau est bien représentée par l'estimation de 1310–1400 °C.

tu.prop: We note that the magmatism of the alkaline trachy basalt, for which we estimated the water content using SIMS, was active ~1000 years ago, and therefore, the present-day mantle potential temperature is well represented by the estimate of 1310–1400 °C.
In addition, an ancient sediment component, most probably in the metasomatised mantle in the MTZ, is also associated with the source18 (Supplementary Fig. S9).

tuv.seg: The source mantle of the Changbaishan basalts is considered to contain some sediment component from the subducted Pacific slab18.
On considère que le manteau source des basaltes de Changbaishan contient des composants sédimentaires provenant de la plaque Pacifique subduite18.

tu.prop: The source mantle of the Changbaishan basalts is considered to contain some sediment component from the subducted Pacific slab18.
This study and earlier works19,22 show the involvement of appreciable amounts of a pyroxenite component of basaltic oceanic crust in origin and H2O in the source mantle.

tuv.seg: In addition, an ancient sediment component, most probably in the metasomatised mantle in the MTZ, is also associated with the source18 (Supplementary Fig. S9).
De plus, un ancien composant sédimentaire très certainement situé dans la zone de transition du manteau métasomatisé est également associé à la source18 (fig. supplémentaire S9).

tu.prop: In addition, an ancient sediment component, most probably in the metasomatised mantle in the MTZ, is also associated with the source18 (Supplementary Fig. S9).

It is unlikely that the basaltic oceanic crust (eclogite) and the sediments were the source of the buoyancy, because eclogite and continental crustal materials are denser than the ambient peridotite in the depth range of the deep upper mantle and MTZ36,37.

tuv.seg: This study and earlier works19,22 show the involvement of appreciable amounts of a pyroxenite component of basaltic oceanic crust in origin and H2O in the source mantle.

La présente étude, soutenue par des travaux antérieurs19,22 démontre que des quantités importantes de H2O et de fragments de pyroxénite originaires de la croûte océanique basaltique sont présentes dans le manteau source.

tu.prop: This study and earlier works19,22 show the involvement of appreciable amounts of a pyroxenite component of basaltic oceanic crust in origin and H2O in the source mantle.
In contrast, hydrous mantle peridotite is less dense than dry mantle peridotite, and the hydrous fluids and partial melts can also be less dense than the host hydrous peridotite in the MTZ34.

tuv.seg: It is unlikely that the basaltic oceanic crust (eclogite) and the sediments were the source of the buoyancy, because eclogite and continental crustal materials are denser than the ambient peridotite in the depth range of the deep upper mantle and MTZ36,37.

Il est peu probable que la croûte océanique basaltique (éclogite) et les sédiments aient été à l’origine de la flottabilité, puisque l’éclogite et les matériaux de la croûte continentale sont plus denses que la péridotite ambiante dans la gamme de profondeur du manteau supérieur profond et de la zone de transition.

tu.prop: It is unlikely that the basaltic oceanic crust (eclogite) and the sediments were the source of the buoyancy, because eclogite and continental crustal materials are denser than the ambient peridotite in the depth range of the deep upper mantle and MTZ36,37.

Therefore, hydrated mantle peridotite beneath the basaltic oceanic crust in the subducted Pacific plate and/or peridotite in the MTZ above the slab are the sources of the compositional buoyancy that formed the hydrous mantle plume beneath Changbaishan (Fig. 2).

tuv.seg: In contrast, hydrous mantle peridotite is less dense than dry mantle peridotite, and the hydrous fluids and partial melts can also be less dense than the host hydrous peridotite in the MTZ34.

En revanche, la péridotite hydratée du manteau est moins dense que la péridotite anhydre. Les fluides hydratés et les liquides issus de la fusion partielle peuvent également être moins denses que la péridotite hôte hydratée dans la zone de transition34.

tu.prop: In contrast, hydrous mantle peridotite is less dense than dry mantle peridotite, and the hydrous fluids and partial melts can also be less dense than the host hydrous peridotite in the MTZ34.

tuv.seg: Therefore, hydrated mantle peridotite beneath the basaltic oceanic crust in the subducted Pacific plate and/or peridotite in the MTZ above the slab are the sources of the compositional buoyancy that formed the hydrous mantle plume beneath Changbaishan (Fig. 2).

Ainsi, la péridotite hydratée du manteau sous la croûte océanique basaltique dans la plaque Pacifique subduite et/ou dans la zone de transition au-dessus de la plaque est à l’origine de la flottabilité compositionnelle qui a provoqué la formation du panache mantellique en dessous de Changbaishan. (Fig. 2)

tu.prop: Therefore, hydrated mantle peridotite beneath the basaltic oceanic crust in the subducted Pacific plate and/or peridotite in the MTZ above the slab are the sources of the compositional buoyancy that formed the hydrous mantle plume beneath Changbaishan (Fig. 2).

Illustration schématique de l'origine du panache mantellique de la zone de transition du manteau hydraté sous le volcan intraplaque Changbaishan.
