tu.prop: Prolonged subduction of the initially cold slab and subsequent storage in the MTZ resulted in thermal relaxation of the slab by conductive heat from the surrounding mantle, which can cause volatilisation of the slab9,38.

tuv.seg: Full size image
Image en grand format

tu.prop: Dehydration of the stagnant Pacific slab in the MTZ is suggested from deep earthquakes within the slab39 (Fig. 1b).

tuv.seg: Prolonged subduction of the initially cold slab and subsequent storage in the MTZ resulted in thermal relaxation of the slab by conductive heat from the surrounding mantle, which can cause volatilisation of the slab9,38.

La subduction prolongée de la plaque initialement froide et l'enfoncement subséquent dans la zone de transition ont provoqué la relaxation thermique de la plaque par conduction de la chaleur provenant du manteau ambiant, ce qui peut causer la volatilisation de la plaque9,38.

tu.prop: Prolonged subduction of the initially cold slab and subsequent storage in the MTZ resulted in thermal relaxation of the slab by conductive heat from the surrounding mantle, which can cause volatilisation of the slab9,38.

The dehydration of the slab peridotite in the MTZ and/or CO2 release from the carbonated basaltic slab could have occurred in the MTZ and played an important role in the rehydration or re-carbonation of the peridotite in the MTZ9,34,35.

tuv.seg: Dehydration of the stagnant Pacific slab in the MTZ is suggested from deep earthquakes within the slab39 (Fig. 1b).
La déshydratation de la plaque Pacifique stagnante dans la zone de transition est suggérée à partir de profonds tremblements de terre au niveau de la plaque39 (fig. 1b).

tu.prop: Dehydration of the stagnant Pacific slab in the MTZ is suggested from deep earthquakes within the slab39 (Fig. 1b).
Electrical conductivity studies have suggested that the MTZ beneath Northeast China is hydrous16 with as much as ~1 wt.% water17.

tuv.seg: The dehydration of the slab peridotite in the MTZ and/or CO2 release from the carbonated basaltic slab could have occurred in the MTZ and played an important role in the rehydration or re-carbonation of the peridotite in the MTZ9,34,35.

La déshydratation de la péridotite de la plaque dans la zone de transition et/ou la libération de CO2 de la plaque basaltique carbonatée aurait pu se produire dans la zone de transition et jouer un rôle important dans la réhydratation ou la recarbonatation de la péridotite dans la zone de transition9,34,35.

tu.prop: The dehydration of the slab peridotite in the MTZ and/or CO2 release from the carbonated basaltic slab could have occurred in the MTZ and played an important role in the rehydration or re-carbonation of the peridotite in the MTZ9,34,35.
A numerical study suggests that a wet peridotite plume could be generated at the top of the MTZ if ~0.5 wt.% water is contained11.

tuv.seg: Electrical conductivity studies have suggested that the MTZ beneath Northeast China is hydrous16 with as much as ~1 wt.% water17.
Des études de conductivité électrique ont suggéré que la zone de transition du manteau sous le nord-est de la Chine est hydratée16, contenant jusqu’à ~1 % en poids d’eau17.

tu.prop: Electrical conductivity studies have suggested that the MTZ beneath Northeast China is hydrous16 with as much as ~1 wt.% water17.

These observations and discussions support the possibility that the Changbaishan mantle plume formed as a result of upwelling of the hydrated (and carbonated) mantle from the MTZ without significant thermal buoyancy, and as such the hydrated mantle contained chemical components from the sediments and the basaltic oceanic crust carried by the hydrous (and perhaps carbonaceous) fluids from the stagnant slab (Fig. 2).

tuv.seg: A numerical study suggests that a wet peridotite plume could be generated at the top of the MTZ if ~0.5 wt.% water is contained11.
Une étude numérique suggère qu’un panache de péridotite humide pourrait être généré au sommet de la zone de transition s'il contient ~0,5 % en poids d’eau11.

tu.prop: A numerical study suggests that a wet peridotite plume could be generated at the top of the MTZ if ~0.5 wt.% water is contained11.

Soon after the ascent of the plume from the MTZ to the upper mantle, dehydration melting should occur because of the reduction in water solubility in the constituent nominally anhydrous minerals40 (Fig. 2).

tuv.seg: These observations and discussions support the possibility that the Changbaishan mantle plume formed as a result of upwelling of the hydrated (and carbonated) mantle from the MTZ without significant thermal buoyancy, and as such the hydrated mantle contained chemical components from the sediments and the basaltic oceanic crust carried by the hydrous (and perhaps carbonaceous) fluids from the stagnant slab (Fig. 2).

Ces observations et discussions appuient l'hypothèse que la formation du panache mantellique de Changbaishan ait résulté de la remontée du manteau hydraté (et carboné) depuis la zone de transition sans convection thermique significative. Ainsi, le manteau hydraté aurait contenu des composés chimiques provenant des sédiments et de la croûte océanique basaltique, transportés par les fluides hydratés (et peut-être carbonés) de la plaque stagnante (Fig. 2).

tu.prop: These observations and discussions support the possibility that the Changbaishan mantle plume formed as a result of upwelling of the hydrated (and carbonated) mantle from the MTZ without significant thermal buoyancy, and as such the hydrated mantle contained chemical components from the sediments and the basaltic oceanic crust carried by the hydrous (and perhaps carbonaceous) fluids from the stagnant slab (Fig. 2).

A prominent low-velocity anomaly is observed at a depth range of 100–410 km beneath Changbaishan (Fig. 1b), suggesting that hydrous (and CO2-bearing) partial melting occurs within the entire depth range of the upwelling plume.

tuv.seg: Soon after the ascent of the plume from the MTZ to the upper mantle, dehydration melting should occur because of the reduction in water solubility in the constituent nominally anhydrous minerals40 (Fig. 2).

Peu de temps après la remontée du panache de la zone de transition au manteau supérieur, une fusion par déshydratation devrait se produire en raison de la réduction de la solubilité de l'eau dans les minéraux constitutifs nominalement anhydres40 (fig. 2).

tu.prop: Soon after the ascent of the plume from the MTZ to the upper mantle, dehydration melting should occur because of the reduction in water solubility in the constituent nominally anhydrous minerals40 (Fig. 2).
The estimated water content in the source mantle for the H2505 alkaline basalt is only 350–550 ppm.

tuv.seg: A prominent low-velocity anomaly is observed at a depth range of 100–410 km beneath Changbaishan (Fig. 1b), suggesting that hydrous (and CO2-bearing) partial melting occurs within the entire depth range of the upwelling plume.

Une importante anomalie de vitesse lente est observée dans une gamme de profondeur de 100 à 410 km sous Changbaishan (fig. 1b), ce qui suggère que la fusion partielle hydratée (et en présence de CO2) se produit sur toute la gamme de profondeur du panache ascendant.

tu.prop: A prominent low-velocity anomaly is observed at a depth range of 100–410 km beneath Changbaishan (Fig. 1b), suggesting that hydrous (and CO2-bearing) partial melting occurs within the entire depth range of the upwelling plume.
The relatively low water content may be because the primary H2505 magma represents the melt segregated from the shallowest part of the ascending plume at ~100 km depth (i.e. 2.6–2.8 GPa).

tuv.seg: The estimated water content in the source mantle for the H2505 alkaline basalt is only 350–550 ppm.
La teneur en eau estimée dans le manteau source pour le basalte alcalin H2505 n'est que de 350 à 550 ppm.

tu.prop: The estimated water content in the source mantle for the H2505 alkaline basalt is only 350–550 ppm.
A more hydrous magma would be generated from deeper levels of the mantle plume.

tuv.seg: The relatively low water content may be because the primary H2505 magma represents the melt segregated from the shallowest part of the ascending plume at ~100 km depth (i.e. 2.6–2.8 GPa).

La teneur relativement faible en eau peut s'expliquer par le fait que le magma primaire H2505 représente la fraction de liquide de la partie la moins profonde du panache ascendant située à ~100 km de profondeur (c'est-à-dire 2,6-2,8 GPa).

tu.prop: The relatively low water content may be because the primary H2505 magma represents the melt segregated from the shallowest part of the ascending plume at ~100 km depth (i.e. 2.6–2.8 GPa).
The majority of the basaltic magmas are alkaline, but tholeiitic basalts also have erupted from the Changbaishan volcano (Supplementary Fig. S1).

tuv.seg: A more hydrous magma would be generated from deeper levels of the mantle plume.
Un magma plus hydraté serait généré à partir des niveaux plus profonds du panache mantellique.

tu.prop: A more hydrous magma would be generated from deeper levels of the mantle plume.

Alkaline basalts and tholeiitic basalts coexist in some Cenozoic intraplate volcanoes in Northeast China, which have been explained by two contrasting scenarios: (1) alkaline basalts formed as a result of partial melting of the asthenospheric mantle and tholeiitic basalts from partial melting of the sub-continental lithospheric mantle heated by asthenospheric melts41, and (2) both alkaline and tholeiitic basalts formed from the same two-lithology plume with different degrees of partial melting of the embedded pyroxenite and surrounding peridotite mantle22.

tuv.seg: The majority of the basaltic magmas are alkaline, but tholeiitic basalts also have erupted from the Changbaishan volcano (Supplementary Fig. S1).
La majorité des magmas basaltiques sont alcalins, mais des basaltes tholéiitiques ont également éclaté du volcan Changbaishan (Fig. Supplémentaire S1).

tu.prop: The majority of the basaltic magmas are alkaline, but tholeiitic basalts also have erupted from the Changbaishan volcano (Supplementary Fig. S1).

The Pb isotopic compositions of the Changbaishan basalts are distinct from the majority composition of the sub-continental lithospheric mantle, and those of the tholeiitic and alkaline basalt compositions essentially overlap (Supplementary Fig. S9).

tuv.seg: Alkaline basalts and tholeiitic basalts coexist in some Cenozoic intraplate volcanoes in Northeast China, which have been explained by two contrasting scenarios: (1) alkaline basalts formed as a result of partial melting of the asthenospheric mantle and tholeiitic basalts from partial melting of the sub-continental lithospheric mantle heated by asthenospheric melts41, and (2) both alkaline and tholeiitic basalts formed from the same two-lithology plume with different degrees of partial melting of the embedded pyroxenite and surrounding peridotite mantle22.

Les basaltes alcalins et les basaltes tholéiitiques coexistent dans certains volcans intraplaques cénozoïques du nord-est de la Chine, qui ont été expliqués par deux scénarios contrastés : (1) des basaltes alcalins formés à la suite de la fusion partielle du manteau asthénosphérique et des basaltes tholéiitiques provenant de la fusion partielle du manteau lithosphérique sous-continental chauffé par des fondus asthénosphériques41, et (2) des basaltes alcalins et tholéiitiques formés à partir du même panache à double lithologie avec différents degrés de fusion partielle de la pyroxénite incorporée et du manteau péridotique environnant22.

tu.prop: Alkaline basalts and tholeiitic basalts coexist in some Cenozoic intraplate volcanoes in Northeast China, which have been explained by two contrasting scenarios: (1) alkaline basalts formed as a result of partial melting of the asthenospheric mantle and tholeiitic basalts from partial melting of the sub-continental lithospheric mantle heated by asthenospheric melts41, and (2) both alkaline and tholeiitic basalts formed from the same two-lithology plume with different degrees of partial melting of the embedded pyroxenite and surrounding peridotite mantle22.
This observation suggests that both the Changbaishan tholeiitic and alkaline basalts derived from the same mantle plume.

tuv.seg: The Pb isotopic compositions of the Changbaishan basalts are distinct from the majority composition of the sub-continental lithospheric mantle, and those of the tholeiitic and alkaline basalt compositions essentially overlap (Supplementary Fig. S9).

Les compositions isotopiques en Pb des basaltes de Changbaishan sont distinctes de la composition majoritaire du manteau lithosphérique sous-continental, et celles des compositions basaltiques tholéiitiques et alcalines coïncident essentiellement (figure supplémentaire S9).

tu.prop: The Pb isotopic compositions of the Changbaishan basalts are distinct from the majority composition of the sub-continental lithospheric mantle, and those of the tholeiitic and alkaline basalt compositions essentially overlap (Supplementary Fig. S9).
This is consistent with the observation that the Fe2O3* contents of the tholeiitic and alkaline basalts are similar at a given MgO content42 (Supplementary Fig. S2).

tuv.seg: This observation suggests that both the Changbaishan tholeiitic and alkaline basalts derived from the same mantle plume.
Cette observation suggère que les basaltes tholéiitiques et alcalins de Changbaishan ont dérivé du même panache mantellique.

tu.prop: This observation suggests that both the Changbaishan tholeiitic and alkaline basalts derived from the same mantle plume.

The tholeiitic basalts should have been derived from a greater degree of melting of the source mantle compared to the alkaline basalts, as has been suggested for the Northeast China intraplate basalts22.

tuv.seg: This is consistent with the observation that the Fe2O3* contents of the tholeiitic and alkaline basalts are similar at a given MgO content42 (Supplementary Fig. S2).
Cela est conforme à l'observation selon laquelle les teneurs en Fe2O3* des basaltes tholéiitiques et alcalins sont similaires pour une teneur en MgO donnée42 (figure supplémentaire S2).

tu.prop: This is consistent with the observation that the Fe2O3* contents of the tholeiitic and alkaline basalts are similar at a given MgO content42 (Supplementary Fig. S2).
Onset of adiabatic melting of the tholeiitic basalt source mantle should be deeper (Fig. 2) because of either a high mantle potential temperature or high water content22.

tuv.seg: The tholeiitic basalts should have been derived from a greater degree of melting of the source mantle compared to the alkaline basalts, as has been suggested for the Northeast China intraplate basalts22.

Les basaltes tholéiitiques auraient dû provenir d'un plus grand degré de fusion du manteau source par rapport aux basaltes alcalins, comme cela a été suggéré pour les basaltes intraplaques du nord-est de la Chine22.

tu.prop: The tholeiitic basalts should have been derived from a greater degree of melting of the source mantle compared to the alkaline basalts, as has been suggested for the Northeast China intraplate basalts22.
Determination of the water content in the tholeiitic basalts is a crucial test for the hydrous mantle plume hypothesis.

tuv.seg: Onset of adiabatic melting of the tholeiitic basalt source mantle should be deeper (Fig. 2) because of either a high mantle potential temperature or high water content22.

Le début de la fusion adiabatique du manteau de la source de basalte tholéiitique doit être plus profond (Fig. 2) en raison d'une température potentielle élevée du manteau ou d'une teneur élevée en eau22.

tu.prop: Onset of adiabatic melting of the tholeiitic basalt source mantle should be deeper (Fig. 2) because of either a high mantle potential temperature or high water content22.
However, tholeiitic basalts younger than 0.5 Ma have not been found43.

tuv.seg: Determination of the water content in the tholeiitic basalts is a crucial test for the hydrous mantle plume hypothesis.
La détermination de la teneur en eau dans les basaltes tholéiitiques est un test crucial pour l'hypothèse du panache du manteau hydraté.

tu.prop: Determination of the water content in the tholeiitic basalts is a crucial test for the hydrous mantle plume hypothesis.
In addition, water content could not be obtained for the tholeiitic basalts because of a lack of quenched samples suitable for water content analysis.

tuv.seg: However, tholeiitic basalts younger than 0.5 Ma have not been found43.
Cependant, les basaltes tholéiitiques inférieurs à 0,5 Ma n'ont pas été trouvés43.

tu.prop: However, tholeiitic basalts younger than 0.5 Ma have not been found43.

Concerning the origin of the Changbaishan mantle plume, one research group has developed a teleseismic tomography model beneath Northeast Asia and suggested that the stagnant slab in the MTZ has a hole beneath Changbaishan, through which hot and buoyant mantle materials ascend from the lower mantle, across the stagnant slab, and reach the upper mantle33.

tuv.seg: In addition, water content could not be obtained for the tholeiitic basalts because of a lack of quenched samples suitable for water content analysis.
De plus, la teneur en eau n'a pas pu être obtenue pour les basaltes tholéiitiques en raison d'un manque d'échantillons trempés adaptés à l'analyse de la teneur en eau.

tu.prop: In addition, water content could not be obtained for the tholeiitic basalts because of a lack of quenched samples suitable for water content analysis.

However, the teleseismic tomography method used has an inherent limitation, i.e. the background mean velocity at each depth in the study volume is removed during the computation of the teleseismic relative travel times which are used as data in the tomographic inversion.

tuv.seg: Concerning the origin of the Changbaishan mantle plume, one research group has developed a teleseismic tomography model beneath Northeast Asia and suggested that the stagnant slab in the MTZ has a hole beneath Changbaishan, through which hot and buoyant mantle materials ascend from the lower mantle, across the stagnant slab, and reach the upper mantle33.

En ce qui concerne l'origine du panache du manteau de Changbaishan, un groupe de recherche a développé un modèle de tomographie télésismique sous l'Asie du Nord-Est et a suggéré que la plaque stagnante dans la zone de transition a un trou sous Changbaishan, à travers lequel les matériaux chauds et flottants du manteau montent du manteau inférieur, à travers la plaque stagnante, et atteignent le manteau supérieur33.

tu.prop: Concerning the origin of the Changbaishan mantle plume, one research group has developed a teleseismic tomography model beneath Northeast Asia and suggested that the stagnant slab in the MTZ has a hole beneath Changbaishan, through which hot and buoyant mantle materials ascend from the lower mantle, across the stagnant slab, and reach the upper mantle33.

Thus, teleseismic tomography alone is unable to recover the real structure of a network-wide feature at a given depth range beneath a seismic network, such as the wide and flat stagnant slab in the MTZ beneath Northeast Asia.

tuv.seg: However, the teleseismic tomography method used has an inherent limitation, i.e. the background mean velocity at each depth in the study volume is removed during the computation of the teleseismic relative travel times which are used as data in the tomographic inversion.

Cependant, la méthode de tomographie télésismique utilisée a une limitation inhérente, c'est-à-dire que la vitesse moyenne de fond à chaque profondeur dans le volume d'étude est supprimée pendant le calcul des temps de trajet relatifs télésismiques qui sont utilisés comme données dans l'inversion tomographique.

tu.prop: However, the teleseismic tomography method used has an inherent limitation, i.e. the background mean velocity at each depth in the study volume is removed during the computation of the teleseismic relative travel times which are used as data in the tomographic inversion.

To resolve this problem, Chen et al.44 developed a new and robust three-dimensional tomographic model of the mantle beneath Northeast Asia by conducting a joint inversion of both local-earthquake arrival times and teleseismic relative travel times.

tuv.seg: Thus, teleseismic tomography alone is unable to recover the real structure of a network-wide feature at a given depth range beneath a seismic network, such as the wide and flat stagnant slab in the MTZ beneath Northeast Asia.

Ainsi, la tomographie télésismique seule est incapable de récupérer la structure réelle d'une fonction à l'échelle du réseau à une plage de profondeur donnée sous un réseau sismique, telle que la plaque stagnante large et plate dans la zone de transition sous l'Asie du Nord-Est.

tu.prop: Thus, teleseismic tomography alone is unable to recover the real structure of a network-wide feature at a given depth range beneath a seismic network, such as the wide and flat stagnant slab in the MTZ beneath Northeast Asia.
Their model shows that there is no hole in the stagnant slab beneath Changbaishan.

tuv.seg: To resolve this problem, Chen et al.44 developed a new and robust three-dimensional tomographic model of the mantle beneath Northeast Asia by conducting a joint inversion of both local-earthquake arrival times and teleseismic relative travel times.

Pour résoudre ce problème, Chen et al. [44] ont développé un nouveau modèle solide tomographique tridimensionnel du manteau sous l'Asie du Nord-Est en effectuant une inversion conjointe des temps d'arrivée des tremblements de terre locaux et des temps de déplacement relatifs télésismiques.

tu.prop: To resolve this problem, Chen et al.44 developed a new and robust three-dimensional tomographic model of the mantle beneath Northeast Asia by conducting a joint inversion of both local-earthquake arrival times and teleseismic relative travel times.

The results of our analyses regarding the potential temperature of the mantle plume does not suggest any advective heat flux through the stagnant slab, and thus it is consistent with the scenario that there is no hole in the stagnant slab.

tuv.seg: Their model shows that there is no hole in the stagnant slab beneath Changbaishan.
Leur modèle montre qu'il n'y a pas de trou dans la plaque stagnante sous Changbaishan.

tu.prop: Their model shows that there is no hole in the stagnant slab beneath Changbaishan.

Recently, Kimura et al.22 proposed that the mantle upwelling beneath Changbaishan reflects a plume that branched off from a lower mantle-derived plume beneath the Datong volcanic field in Northeast China (Fig. 1), which migrated horizontally over the stagnant slab to the east and penetrated into the upper mantle during the opening of the Sea of Japan at 30–15 Ma caused by the slab rollback of the Pacific Plate.

tuv.seg: The results of our analyses regarding the potential temperature of the mantle plume does not suggest any advective heat flux through the stagnant slab, and thus it is consistent with the scenario that there is no hole in the stagnant slab.

Les résultats de nos analyses concernant la température potentielle du panache mantellique ne suggèrent aucun flux de chaleur advective à travers la plaque stagnante, l'absence de trou dans la plaque stagnante est donc cohérente avec le scénario.

tu.prop: The results of our analyses regarding the potential temperature of the mantle plume does not suggest any advective heat flux through the stagnant slab, and thus it is consistent with the scenario that there is no hole in the stagnant slab.
However, the current volcanic activity at Datong is much weaker than that at Changbaishan.

tuv.seg: Recently, Kimura et al.22 proposed that the mantle upwelling beneath Changbaishan reflects a plume that branched off from a lower mantle-derived plume beneath the Datong volcanic field in Northeast China (Fig. 1), which migrated horizontally over the stagnant slab to the east and penetrated into the upper mantle during the opening of the Sea of Japan at 30–15 Ma caused by the slab rollback of the Pacific Plate.

Récemment, Kimura et al. [22] ont proposé que la remontée du manteau sous Changbaishan reflète un panache qui a bifurqué à partir d'un panache d'origine mantellique inférieur sous le champ volcanique de Datong dans le nord-est de la Chine (Fig. 1), qui a migré horizontalement sur la dalle stagnante à l'est et a pénétré dans le manteau supérieur lors de l'ouverture de la mer du Japon à 30–15 Ma causée par le recul de la dalle de la plaque du Pacifique.

tu.prop: Recently, Kimura et al.22 proposed that the mantle upwelling beneath Changbaishan reflects a plume that branched off from a lower mantle-derived plume beneath the Datong volcanic field in Northeast China (Fig. 1), which migrated horizontally over the stagnant slab to the east and penetrated into the upper mantle during the opening of the Sea of Japan at 30–15 Ma caused by the slab rollback of the Pacific Plate.

In addition, the present-day low-VP plume structure at depths of ~300–410 km extends its trail to the east (indicated by the arrow in Fig. 1b) under which deep earthquakes in the MTZ have occurred in the Pacific slab.

tuv.seg: However, the current volcanic activity at Datong is much weaker than that at Changbaishan.
Cependant, l'activité volcanique actuelle à Datong est beaucoup plus faible que celle de Changbaishan.

tu.prop: However, the current volcanic activity at Datong is much weaker than that at Changbaishan.
This observation suggests that dehydration of the stagnant slab, associated with deep earthquake faulting, is linked to the generation of the hydrous mantle plume beneath Changbaishan39 (Fig. 2).

tuv.seg: In addition, the present-day low-VP plume structure at depths of ~300–410 km extends its trail to the east (indicated by the arrow in Fig. 1b) under which deep earthquakes in the MTZ have occurred in the Pacific slab.

En outre, la structure actuelle de panache à faible VP à des profondeurs d'environ 300 à 410 km étend son sentier vers l'est (indiqué par la flèche sur la figure 1b) sous lequel de profonds tremblements de terre dans la zone de transition se sont produits dans la plaque du Pacifique.

tu.prop: In addition, the present-day low-VP plume structure at depths of ~300–410 km extends its trail to the east (indicated by the arrow in Fig. 1b) under which deep earthquakes in the MTZ have occurred in the Pacific slab.

Recent petrological studies have suggested that some hydrous magmas from the Siberian Traps LIP (252–250 Ma) may have occurred as a result of mantle plumes derived from the hydrous MTZ9, while hydrous LIP magmatism at Tarim (~290 Ma) and Emeishan (~260 Ma) is considered to have been sourced from hydrous materials from the MTZ entrained by a deep hot plume source45.

tuv.seg: This observation suggests that dehydration of the stagnant slab, associated with deep earthquake faulting, is linked to the generation of the hydrous mantle plume beneath Changbaishan39 (Fig. 2).

Cette observation suggère que la déshydratation de la plaque stagnante, associée à des failles sismiques profondes, est liée à la génération du panache mantellique hydrique sous Changbaishan [39] (Données 2).

tu.prop: This observation suggests that dehydration of the stagnant slab, associated with deep earthquake faulting, is linked to the generation of the hydrous mantle plume beneath Changbaishan39 (Fig. 2).

In the ancient Earth, the mantle was hotter and significant water could not have been transported to the Earth’s deep interior via subduction of the oceanic plates because of the total breakdown of the hydrous minerals in the slab46 and low water solubility in the nominally anhydrous minerals in the mantle47.

tuv.seg: Recent petrological studies have suggested that some hydrous magmas from the Siberian Traps LIP (252–250 Ma) may have occurred as a result of mantle plumes derived from the hydrous MTZ9, while hydrous LIP magmatism at Tarim (~290 Ma) and Emeishan (~260 Ma) is considered to have been sourced from hydrous materials from the MTZ entrained by a deep hot plume source45.

Des études pétrologiques récentes ont suggéré que certains magmas hydratés de la province magmatique des pièges sibériens (252-250 Ma) ont pu se produire à la suite de panaches mantelliques dérivés de la zone de transition du manteau9 hydrique, tandis que le magmatisme de la province magmatique hydrique à Tarim (~290 Ma) et Emeishan (~260 Ma) est considéré comme provenant de matériaux hydratés de la MTZ entraînés par une source de panaches chauds profonds [45].

tu.prop: Recent petrological studies have suggested that some hydrous magmas from the Siberian Traps LIP (252–250 Ma) may have occurred as a result of mantle plumes derived from the hydrous MTZ9, while hydrous LIP magmatism at Tarim (~290 Ma) and Emeishan (~260 Ma) is considered to have been sourced from hydrous materials from the MTZ entrained by a deep hot plume source45.

However, the mantle would have been sufficiently cool after ~1 Ga for water to be effectively transported to the MTZ18, and the MTZ would have become locally hydrated because of dehydration/decarbonation of the stagnant slabs48.

tuv.seg: In the ancient Earth, the mantle was hotter and significant water could not have been transported to the Earth’s deep interior via subduction of the oceanic plates because of the total breakdown of the hydrous minerals in the slab46 and low water solubility in the nominally anhydrous minerals in the mantle47.

Dans la Terre ancienne, le manteau était plus chaud et une quantité importante d'eau n'aurait pas pu être transportée vers l'intérieur profond de la Terre par subduction des plaques océaniques en raison de la dégradation totale des minéraux hydratés dans la dalle [46] et de la faible solubilité de l'eau dans les minéraux nominalement anhydres du manteau [47].

tu.prop: In the ancient Earth, the mantle was hotter and significant water could not have been transported to the Earth’s deep interior via subduction of the oceanic plates because of the total breakdown of the hydrous minerals in the slab46 and low water solubility in the nominally anhydrous minerals in the mantle47.
The mantle plumes of the hydrous Siberian Traps magmatism could also have been generated from hydrous plumes from an intensively hydrated MTZ9 without significant excessive heat.

tuv.seg: However, the mantle would have been sufficiently cool after ~1 Ga for water to be effectively transported to the MTZ18, and the MTZ would have become locally hydrated because of dehydration/decarbonation of the stagnant slabs48.

Cependant, le manteau aurait été suffisamment froid après ~1 Ga pour que l'eau soit efficacement transportée vers la zone de transition18, qui se serait hydraté localement en raison de la déshydratation/décarbonatation des dalles stagnantes [48].

tu.prop: However, the mantle would have been sufficiently cool after ~1 Ga for water to be effectively transported to the MTZ18, and the MTZ would have become locally hydrated because of dehydration/decarbonation of the stagnant slabs48.
Our study of the Changbaishan alkaline basalts suggests the important role of water from the stagnant slab in the MTZ as the source of the formation of the hydrous mantle plume.

tuv.seg: The mantle plumes of the hydrous Siberian Traps magmatism could also have been generated from hydrous plumes from an intensively hydrated MTZ9 without significant excessive heat.

Les panaches mantelliques du magmatisme des pièges sibériens hydratés pourraient également avoir été générés à partir de panaches hydratés provenant d'une zone de transition du manteau9 intensément hydratée sans chaleur excessive significative.

tu.prop: The mantle plumes of the hydrous Siberian Traps magmatism could also have been generated from hydrous plumes from an intensively hydrated MTZ9 without significant excessive heat.
The roles of the hydrous mantle in the MTZ might vary and shed new light on the discussion of the origin of hydrous basalts in intra-continental basalt fields and continental LIPs.

tuv.seg: Our study of the Changbaishan alkaline basalts suggests the important role of water from the stagnant slab in the MTZ as the source of the formation of the hydrous mantle plume.

Notre étude des basaltes alcalins de Changbaishan suggère le rôle important de l'eau de la plaque stagnante dans la zone de transition du manteau comme source de la formation du panache du manteau hydraté.

tu.prop: Our study of the Changbaishan alkaline basalts suggests the important role of water from the stagnant slab in the MTZ as the source of the formation of the hydrous mantle plume.

tuv.seg: The roles of the hydrous mantle in the MTZ might vary and shed new light on the discussion of the origin of hydrous basalts in intra-continental basalt fields and continental LIPs.

Les rôles du manteau hydraté dans la one de transition pourraient varier et apporter un nouvel éclairage sur la discussion de l'origine des basaltes hydratés dans les champs de basaltes intra-continentaux et les provinces magmatiques (LIP).

tu.prop: The roles of the hydrous mantle in the MTZ might vary and shed new light on the discussion of the origin of hydrous basalts in intra-continental basalt fields and continental LIPs.

tuv.seg: Methods
Méthodes

tu.prop: Concentrations of whole-rock major elements and some trace elements (Sc, V, Cr, Co, and Ni) were determined using X-ray fluorescence (XRF) using a Spectoris MagiX PRO at the Graduate School of Science, Hokkaido University.

tuv.seg: Whole-rock compositional analyses
Analyses de composition de roches entières

tu.prop: Additional trace elements were analysed using inductively coupled plasma mass spectrometry (ICP–MS), using a Thermo Fisher Scientific X-series instrument at Hokkaido University.

tuv.seg: Concentrations of whole-rock major elements and some trace elements (Sc, V, Cr, Co, and Ni) were determined using X-ray fluorescence (XRF) using a Spectoris MagiX PRO at the Graduate School of Science, Hokkaido University.

Les concentrations d'éléments majeurs de la roche entière et de certains oligo-éléments (Sc, V, Cr, Co et Ni) ont été déterminées en utilisant la fluorescence X (XRF) à l'aide d'un SPECTORIS MAGIX PRO à l'école doctorale des Sciences, Université d'Hokkaido.

tu.prop: Concentrations of whole-rock major elements and some trace elements (Sc, V, Cr, Co, and Ni) were determined using X-ray fluorescence (XRF) using a Spectoris MagiX PRO at the Graduate School of Science, Hokkaido University.
Sr, Nd, and Pb isotopic ratios were determined using a multiple collector (MC)–ICP–MS (Neptune plus, Thermo Fisher Scientific) at Hokkaido University.

tuv.seg: Additional trace elements were analysed using inductively coupled plasma mass spectrometry (ICP–MS), using a Thermo Fisher Scientific X-series instrument at Hokkaido University.

Des éléments-traces supplémentaires ont été analysés à l'aide de la spectrométrie de masse à plasma à couplage inductif (ICP–MS), à l'aide d'un instrument Thermo Fisher Scientific X-series à l'Université d'Hokkaido.

tu.prop: Additional trace elements were analysed using inductively coupled plasma mass spectrometry (ICP–MS), using a Thermo Fisher Scientific X-series instrument at Hokkaido University.
The details of the analytical procedures, including those of the chemical separations and data correction, are described in ref.49.

tuv.seg: Sr, Nd, and Pb isotopic ratios were determined using a multiple collector (MC)–ICP–MS (Neptune plus, Thermo Fisher Scientific) at Hokkaido University.
Les rapports isotopiques Sr, Nd et Pb ont été déterminés à l'aide d'un collecteur multiple (MC)–ICP–MS (Neptune plus, Thermo Fisher Scientific) à l'Université d'Hokkaido.

tu.prop: Sr, Nd, and Pb isotopic ratios were determined using a multiple collector (MC)–ICP–MS (Neptune plus, Thermo Fisher Scientific) at Hokkaido University.
The composition of the Geological Survey of Japan reference material JB-3 was measured during the course of this study; the measured and reference values are listed in Table A1 of ref.49.

tuv.seg: The details of the analytical procedures, including those of the chemical separations and data correction, are described in ref.49.
Les détails des procédures analytiques, y compris celles des séparations chimiques et de la correction des données, sont décrits dans la référence 49.

tu.prop: The details of the analytical procedures, including those of the chemical separations and data correction, are described in ref.49.

tuv.seg: The composition of the Geological Survey of Japan reference material JB-3 was measured during the course of this study; the measured and reference values are listed in Table A1 of ref.49.

La composition du matériau de référence JB-3 de la Commission géologique du Japon a été mesurée au cours de cette étude ; les valeurs mesurées et de référence sont énumérées dans le tableau A1 de la référence 49.

tu.prop: The composition of the Geological Survey of Japan reference material JB-3 was measured during the course of this study; the measured and reference values are listed in Table A1 of ref.49.
Compositions of minerals and glass were determined using a JEOL JXA-8800 electron microprobe at Hokkaido University.

tuv.seg: Electron microprobe analyses
Analyses de microsondes électroniques

tu.prop: For olivine, an accelerating voltage of 15 kV; a beam current of 20 nA; peak and background counting times of 20 s and 10 s, respectively; and a focused beam were adopted.

tuv.seg: Compositions of minerals and glass were determined using a JEOL JXA-8800 electron microprobe at Hokkaido University.
Les compositions de minéraux et de verre ont été déterminées à l'aide d'une microsonde électronique JEOL JXA-8800 à l'Université d'Hokkaido.

tu.prop: Compositions of minerals and glass were determined using a JEOL JXA-8800 electron microprobe at Hokkaido University.
Operating conditions for plagioclase were an accelerating voltage of 15 kV; a beam current of 10 nA; peak and background counting times of 10 s and 5 s, respectively; and a beam diameter of 10 μm.

tuv.seg: For olivine, an accelerating voltage of 15 kV; a beam current of 20 nA; peak and background counting times of 20 s and 10 s, respectively; and a focused beam were adopted.

Pour l'olivine, une tension d'accélération de 15 kV, un courant de faisceau de 20 nA, des temps de comptage de crête et de fond de 20 s et 10 s, respectivement, et un faisceau focalisé ont été adoptés.

tu.prop: For olivine, an accelerating voltage of 15 kV; a beam current of 20 nA; peak and background counting times of 20 s and 10 s, respectively; and a focused beam were adopted.
Operating conditions for glass were an accelerating voltage of 15 kV; a beam current of 10 nA; peak and background counting times of 10 s and 5 s, respectively; and a beam diameter of 30–50 μm.

tuv.seg: Operating conditions for plagioclase were an accelerating voltage of 15 kV; a beam current of 10 nA; peak and background counting times of 10 s and 5 s, respectively; and a beam diameter of 10 μm.

Les conditions de fonctionnement du plagioclase étaient une tension d'accélération de 15 kV, un courant de faisceau de 10 nA, des temps de comptage de crête et de fond de 10 s et 5 s, respectivement, et un diamètre de faisceau de 10 μm.

tu.prop: Operating conditions for plagioclase were an accelerating voltage of 15 kV; a beam current of 10 nA; peak and background counting times of 10 s and 5 s, respectively; and a beam diameter of 10 μm.
Both oxide and natural mineral standards were used, and data were obtained using the ZAF correction method.

tuv.seg: Operating conditions for glass were an accelerating voltage of 15 kV; a beam current of 10 nA; peak and background counting times of 10 s and 5 s, respectively; and a beam diameter of 30–50 μm.

Les conditions de fonctionnement du verre étaient une tension d'accélération de 15 kV, un courant de faisceau de 10 nA, des temps de comptage de crête et de fond de 10 s et 5 s, respectivement, et un diamètre de faisceau de 30–50 μm.

tu.prop: Operating conditions for glass were an accelerating voltage of 15 kV; a beam current of 10 nA; peak and background counting times of 10 s and 5 s, respectively; and a beam diameter of 30–50 μm.

tuv.seg: Both oxide and natural mineral standards were used, and data were obtained using the ZAF correction method.
Des étalons d'oxydes et de minéraux naturels ont été utilisés, et les données ont été obtenues en utilisant la méthode de correction ZAF.

tu.prop: Both oxide and natural mineral standards were used, and data were obtained using the ZAF correction method.

Disponibilité des données

Les ensembles de données générés et/ou analysés au cours de la présente étude sont disponibles auprès des auteurs correspondants.
